Education & Capacity Building

Shaping the next generation of
infrastructure decision-makers

Our multiplier for systemic change. Through professional certifications, executive workshops, and academic partnerships, we empower a global network of practitioners and decision-makers to deliver sustainable, resilient infrastructure.

SDG Alignment 4 Quality Education 9 Industry & Innovation 11 Sustainable Cities

Education & Capacity Building Programs

From postgraduate degrees to executive immersions

International Professional Master's Program (iPMP) at NAMTECH

Industry-focused postgraduate program at NAMTECH in collaboration with TUM (Technical University of Munich), preparing engineering graduates for Industry 4.0 roles in Smart Manufacturing, AI, and Sustainability. The curriculum spans four semesters, beginning with a strong foundation and progressing to advanced concepts, a summer internship, and an industry Capstone Project. Meta-skills are intentionally integrated throughout.

Project / University Partner(s) TUM (Munich) · NAMTECH · Festo Singapore
Location Ahmedabad, India & Singapore
Techno ManagerIndustry 4.0CurriculumSustainability

SE Asia Transport Project Preparatory — Rolling Stock Capacity Building

Six-day study tour developed for the Asian Development Bank covering Rolling Stock design and performance (MRT system), operation, communication and control, maintenance (corrective, routine, predictive), Metro/LRT depot facilities, OCC (GoA4), and manufacturing. Site visits include MRT Depot, MRT Workshop, and LRT Workshop in Singapore.

Project / University Partner(s) TUM · Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location Singapore
AuthorityIndustryRailwayOperation

International Immersion Program — President University MBA

One-week Singapore immersion for MBA students designed in collaboration with President University. Lectures by TUM Professors and industrial experts covering: Circular Economy in Sustainable Infrastructure, Industrial IoT for Smart Manufacturing, Data Analytics & AI, and Advanced Robotics. Industrial visits to ARTC, Yamazaki Mazak iSMART Factory, Siemens AMTC, and Hyundai HMGICS.

Project / University Partner(s) TUM (Munich) · President University
Location Singapore
InternationalSustainabilityGlobalization

Executive Education — Summer School Program Capacity Building

Intensive summer school for international experts and decision-makers, featuring lectures by TUM academics and industry pioneers on cutting-edge theories and strategic frameworks. Extended beyond the classroom with industrial visits to flagship innovation hubs — including the Singapore Rail Test Centre (SRTC) and Hyundai HMGICS — fostering a powerful network of future-oriented leaders.

Project / University Partner(s) TUM Munich
Location Singapore
EducationLeadershipDigital Transformation
Global Outreach

Guest lectures & academic engagements

Sharing knowledge across continents — from Indonesia and India to China and Thailand.

Indonesia

President University

Guest lecture on Planning Framework & Financing for Sustainable E-Mobility in Public Transport and Logistics, delivered to MBA students.

India

NAMTECH, Ahmedabad

Interactive workshop session with iPMP cohort students covering Industry 4.0 topics including Smart Manufacturing and AI-driven sustainability.

Vietnam

Vietnamese-German University (VGU)

Guest lecture session engaging a diverse cohort of engineering and management students on sustainable infrastructure and green mobility.

India

Coimbatore Institute of Technology

Guest lecture on Sustainable Construction, hosted by the Department of Civil Engineering. Presented by Dr.-Ing. Ali Bawono, PMP.

India

Viswajyothi College of Engineering & Technology

Lecture session delivered to a large auditorium of undergraduate engineering students on resilient and sustainable infrastructure practices.

China

Shenzhen Technology University

Lecture on planning the future of electromobility and achieving sustainable transport, attended by engineering students from SZTU.

Indonesia

Petra Christian University

Interactive guest lecture at Petra Christian University, Surabaya, engaging civil and transport engineering students on green urban mobility.

Indonesia

Institut Teknologi Bandung (ITB)

Panel participation at the ICESCO–SCCIC ITB International Workshop on Smart Cities and Circular Green Economy 2023, Bandung.

China

Shenzhen Technology University

Group photo with SZTU students following an engaging session on sustainable transport and smart infrastructure development.

Indonesia

Universitas Negeri Surabaya (UNESA)

Seminar on Green Construction Materials for Sustainable and Resilient Infrastructure, co-presented with TUM Asia at Universitas Negeri Surabaya.

India

PSG College of Technology

Guest lecture at PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore, reaching a large audience of engineering students on sustainable construction and smart infrastructure.

Thailand

TGGS — The Sirindhorn International Thai-German Graduate School

Lecture session at TGGS Bangkok, engaging graduate students and faculty on sustainable infrastructure and digital transformation in transport systems.
